plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-580 RP18.P08.M02.S05-RP18.P08.M09.S02 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-580 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P08 implementation review slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (the sixteen remaining rows), the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, and M08 Hermes Evidence Packet cycles, and the M09 Claude Review Packet head (the first two rows) as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-581 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-580 (Risk C, 150 units, RP18.P08.M02.S05-RP18.P08.M09.S02) is a descriptor-only RP18.P08 implementation review slice and passes independent read-only review. Registry binding, service descriptor/case set, validators, contract projection, package tests, and the rp18 validate script are mutually consistent: the deliverable distribution (hermes_evidence 68 / claude_review 7 / implementation 68 / test 7) and the micro-phase (M02=16, M03-M08=22 each, M09=2) and micro-title counts reconcile exactly to 150, and the M02 tail correctly resumes at S05 (Fixture summary receipt) with no gap/overlap versus CP00-579. Descriptor-only and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code, not merely asserted: freezeCp580Result sets every runtime/write/leak flag false, and validators assert descriptor_only/runtime_execution/raw_payload_included/customer_safe per row; the validate script deepEquals contract<->registry<->service so any drift would fail. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver (allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation), and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false; production_ready_candidate, not a production claim). The pack does not over-claim or weaken any gate: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review, and all listed validation commands are recorded passing (exit 0). Review setup is CP178+ hardened (auth preflight passed with valid JSON; forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields). Two P3 informational notes only; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Final enterprise-trust closeout still requires the accepted Claude review result, adjudication, construction inspection, and human approval, which are intentionally outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review evidence files referenced by manifest are not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: The manifest gate evidence_refs and top-level evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-580/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist yet; artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-580/ similarly has no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json. This is the correct pre-review state, not an over-claim: manifest.status is implemented_pending_review, production_ready and runtime_ready are false, and command-evidence.json explicitly marks these as post-review gated outputs produced from the accepted review.
findings.recommendation: After this review is accepted, generate the post-review artifacts and run the post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:review:run/normalize/receipt:validate, closeout-pack:validate, git diff --check --cached) before commit/closeout so the referenced evidence files actually exist.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-review state; not a defect. No action required at this review stage; tracked for final closeout.

findings.title: Unrelated dirty working-tree files correctly scoped out of CP00-580
findings.details: scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs and tools/progress-control-room/index.html are modified in the working tree but are classified under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and are absent from active_pack_implementation_refs. They belong to the non-CP UI/progress-control-room track and are not part of CP00-580; the review prompt directs that unrelated dirty files not be inferred into pack scope. The eight active pack implementation refs (contract, README, index/registry/service/validators, model.test.js, validate-rp18 script) are the only files in scope.
findings.recommendation: Keep these two files out of the CP00-580 closeout commit and commit them on the separate UI/non-CP track; verify the staged diff (git diff --check --cached) contains only the eight active pack implementation refs.
findings.post_review_disposition: Correctly scoped out by changed-file-scope.json; flagged for the committer to preserve a clean commit boundary. Non-blocking.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-580 ai legal workflows core p08 implementation review slice descriptor pack
